features.featureTitle: Create a `useStableActor` hook in `frontend/src/hooks/useStableActor.ts` that accepts the raw actor from `useActor()`, stabilizes the reference using `useRef` so it only updates once when the raw actor first becomes available, and returns both the stable actor instance and an `isActorFetching` boolean status. This prevents downstream React Query effects from re-running due to raw actor reference changes.

Create a generic `useStableActorQuery` hook in `frontend/src/hooks/useStableActorQuery.ts` that wraps React Query's `useQuery`. It must: accept a selector function `(actor) => Promise<T>`, a `queryKey` array, and an optional `options` object; use `useStableActor` internally; only enable the query when the stable actor is available (`enabled: !!stableActor`); merge `isActorFetching` with the query's own `isLoading` so the returned `isLoading` is `true` during both actor initialization and query fetching; and return the full React Query result object with the enhanced `isLoading`.

Refactor all public page GET query hooks to use `useStableActorQuery` while maintaining identical external interfaces. The affected hooks and their files are: `useGetAllCategories` and `useGetHomepageCategories` (homepage/side panel), `useHomepageAutocomplete` in `frontend/src/hooks/useHomepageAutocomplete.ts`, `useGetCategoryById` (category page), `useCategoryProductsInfinite` in `frontend/src/hooks/useCategoryProductsInfinite.ts` (note: uses `useInfiniteQuery` — adapt pattern accordingly), `useGetProduct` and `useGetProductPhoto` in `frontend/src/hooks/useProductQueries.ts`, and `useGetBothStoreDetails` / `useBothStoreDetails` in `frontend/src/hooks/useBothStoreDetails.ts`. For each hook: replace direct `useActor()` + `enabled: !!actor` pattern with `useStableActorQuery`; define a named selector function for the actor method; preserve all existing query keys, `staleTime`, `gcTime`, retry settings, and error handling; keep all Spanish-language error messages unchanged.

Refactor all admin page GET query hooks to use the `useStableActorQuery` pattern while maintaining identical external interfaces. The affected files include: `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` (category CRUD get hooks), `frontend/src/hooks/useProductQueries.ts` (admin product get hooks), `frontend/src/hooks/useSaleItemQueries.ts` (sale item get hooks), `frontend/src/hooks/useStoreDetailsQueries.ts` (store details get hooks), `frontend/src/hooks/useAdminUserManagement.ts` (user list get hook), `frontend/src/hooks/useAdminExport.ts` (export stats get hook), `frontend/src/hooks/usePublicCategories.ts`, `frontend/src/hooks/useCategoryProductsPaginated.ts`, and `frontend/src/hooks/useProductSearchForSales.ts`. For each GET query hook in these files: replace the direct `useActor()` + `enabled: !!actor` pattern with `useStableActorQuery` (or stable actor gating for `useInfiniteQuery` variants); define named selector functions; preserve all query keys, cache settings, and Spanish error messages.

Ensure `useHomepageCategoriesInfinite` in `frontend/src/hooks/useHomepageCategoriesInfinite.ts` and `useCategoryProductsInfinite` in `frontend/src/hooks/useCategoryProductsInfinite.ts` are adapted for the stable actor pattern using `useStableActor` to gate the `enabled` option on `useInfiniteQuery`, since `useStableActorQuery` wraps `useQuery` only. The stable actor reference must be obtained via `useStableActor`, passed directly into the `queryFn`, and the query enabled only when `!!stableActor`. All existing pagination behavior, `getNextPageParam`, flattened page data, and `fetchNextPage` must be preserved.
